title: campanula baumgartenii subsp. baumgartenii
intro: Campanula baumgartenii subsp. baumgartenii, also known as Baumgarten's bellflower, is a type of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Campanula baumgartenii subsp. baumgartenii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
